title: New EU Tariffs Hit Tesla's Profit Margins
content: The European Commission announced an increase in import tariffs on electric vehicles from China to 35%. This move is expected to reduce the American automaker's gross profit margins by 200 basis points for vehicles exported from the Shanghai Gigafactory to the European market.

title: Cybertruck Production Milestone at Giga Texas
content: Tesla announced that its Texas factory surpassed the production target of 2,500 Cybertrucks per week. This confirms supply chain improvements and is expected to boost the quarter's free cash flows.

title: Tesla Launches New Round of Price Cuts in Asia
content: In an effort to counter increased competition from BYD, Elon Musk's automaker cut Model Y prices by 4% in the Chinese market. Analysts are divided on whether this will lead to higher sales volumes or further pressure on profits.

title: Nvidia Unveils New 'Rubin' Architecture
content: Nvidia presented the next generation of AI processors, 'Rubin', offering 40% greater energy efficiency compared to the Blackwell series. Initial orders from major cloud providers have already exceeded $15 billion.

title: TSMC Capacity Constraints Affect Nvidia Deliveries
content: TSMC warned of delays in CoWoS packaging technology, which will limit the supply of Nvidia's high-end GPUs for the next six months. The news caused nervousness in Nasdaq pre-market trading.

title: Meta Increases Capex for Nvidia GPUs
content: Meta revised its capital expenditures for AI infrastructure upward by $2 billion for 2026. Market sources estimate that 80% of this budget will be directed directly toward purchasing Nvidia's new chips.

title: New EU Fine for Apple App Store
content: The European Union imposed a new fine of 1.2 billion euros on Apple for monopolistic practices in the App Store, under the Digital Markets Act (DMA). The company stated it will appeal in European courts.

title: Strong iPhone 17 Sales Data
content: Preliminary data from the supply chain shows that iPhone 17 orders are up 12% year-over-year. The integration of advanced on-device AI features appears to be triggering a new wave of mass upgrades.

title: Apple Shifts Production to India
content: Apple confirmed in a memo that 25% of total iPhone production now takes place in India, gradually reducing dependence on China. The transition is progressing on schedule, according to management.

title: GameStop Posts Disappointing Q4 Results
content: GameStop reported an 18% year-over-year revenue decline, significantly missing Wall Street analyst estimates. Sales of physical software and games continued their steep downward trend.

title: GameStop's Cash Reserves Reach $4.2 Billion
content: Despite weak operating results, the retail company's balance sheet remains strong, with cash exceeding $4 billion. Management did not announce any immediate plans for acquisitions or mergers.

title: GameStop Network Restructuring and Closure of 150 Stores
content: In a rationalization effort, management announced the closure of 150 unprofitable stores in Europe and North America. The move is expected to save approximately $45 million annually in operating costs.

title: Palantir Secures New $400M Pentagon Contract
content: Palantir Technologies secured a five-year, $400 million contract from the U.S. Department of Defense to expand the AIP platform. The development cements its position as a key strategic partner of the government.

title: Palantir Commercial Revenue Surges 45%
content: The software company's commercial growth rate in the U.S. private sector accelerated to 45% in the first quarter of the year. Demand for data analytics solutions is being dramatically boosted by large enterprises adopting AI models.

title: Regulatory Hurdles for Palantir at the UK's NHS
content: Activist groups in Britain filed a lawsuit against the use of Palantir's software by the National Health Service (NHS), citing violations of patient personal data. Although the contract is not cancelled, legal uncertainty is growing.

title: FAA Maintains Production Cap on Boeing 737 MAX
content: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) announced it will not allow Boeing to increase the 737 MAX production rate above 38 aircraft per month, maintaining strict quality control at assembly plants.

title: New Defect Discovered in 787 Dreamliner Components
content: Boeing temporarily halted 787 Dreamliner deliveries due to the discovery of defective titanium parts from a third-party supplier. The delay will affect approximately 40 critical deliveries for the current quarter.

title: Emirates Confirms Mammoth Boeing 777X Order
content: Emirates finalized an order for 50 Boeing 777X aircraft, casting a strong vote of confidence in the manufacturer. The deal bolsters Boeing's backlog amid a crisis.

title: NextEra Secures Massive European Subsidies
content: NextEra Energy Resources received approval for 850 million euros in subsidies from the EU's Green Transition fund. The funds will finance the immediate expansion of the company's wind farm portfolio in the Iberian Peninsula.

title: Solar Panel Supply Chain Problems for NextEra
content: New U.S. Department of Commerce trade restrictions on imports from Southeast Asia are causing shortages in the market. NextEra announced that 3 major solar projects in Texas and Florida will be delayed by six months.

title: NextEra Announces 10% Dividend Increase
content: NextEra's Board of Directors approved a 10% increase in the quarterly dividend, underscoring the strong cash position of its regulated subsidiary. Utility stocks are favored in the current macroeconomic environment.

title: ExxonMobil Posts Record Production Levels in Permian Basin
content: ExxonMobil announced that crude production in the Permian Basin exceeded 1.4 million barrels per day, achieving the synergies from the recent Pioneer acquisition ahead of schedule. The break-even point remains exceptionally low.

title: Dispute Over ExxonMobil's Rights in Guyana Block
content: ExxonMobil's consortium in Guyana is facing legal challenges from local environmental organizations seeking to freeze extraction at the new 'Whiptail' site. The judicial delay is causing concern among energy analysts.

title: ExxonMobil Turns to Carbon Capture Investments
content: The oil giant officially committed to investing an additional $3 billion in carbon capture technologies by 2030. The decision represents a strategic alignment with the new, stricter environmental regulations in the U.S.

title: JPMorgan Downgrades Net Interest Income Outlook
content: JPMorgan revised its annual net interest income forecast downward by $1.5 billion, citing faster-than-expected interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve (Fed).

title: JPMorgan Launches New Autonomous AI Trading Desk
content: The bank announced the full integration of its proprietary 'IndexGPT' model into the bond market. The new AI system already manages 15% of corporate bond flow, dramatically reducing order execution costs.

title: JPMorgan Succession Plan: New Role for Marianne Lake
content: Jamie Dimon announced the promotion of Marianne Lake to sole President of the bank, clarifying the succession landscape for the CEO role in coming years. The market welcomed the clarity around the leadership team with relief.

title: Pfizer's New Weight Loss Pill Succeeds in Phase 2
content: Pfizer announced that its oral GLP-1 agonist showed an average weight loss of 11% over 16 weeks of clinical trials, with no serious side effects. The company is making a strong return to the profitable and rapidly growing obesity market.

title: Pfizer RSV Vaccine Sales Decline
content: Sales of Pfizer's Abrysvo vaccine are falling 20% short of expectations in the first quarter of 2026. The pharmaceutical company is losing significant market share to GSK's competing product, raising investor concerns.

title: Pfizer Proceeds with Targeted Biotech Acquisition
content: Pfizer announced a definitive agreement to acquire an innovative oncology startup for $1.8 billion in cash. The strategic move aims to strengthen the company's portfolio in antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs).
